text:
Once upon an ancient time when the world was still settling into various forms of existence, there swirled an ill-fated tale told not just in whispers but among the stones themselves. It was found in the hidden recesses of an old, forgotten crypt, where time seemed to stand still and decayed parchment bore the secrets of a forgotten civilization.

In this place, named Chronos' Fall, a tragic story unfolded. Chronos, revered but tragically misunderstood, sought to unify the fractured lands of Elysium by extending his dominion across time itself. He envisioned a realm where moments could never pass away, where history flowed endlessly forward, and his name etched into eternity.

His project, however, was flawed. Chronos underestimated the nature of time's flow and the power of change inherent within it. In his infinite ambition, he created a singular point that tried to tether all existence into one moment, ceasing all motion.

The result was catastrophic. Temples crumbled instantly,
